title: Sarga 92
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Having quickly sent forth all those arrangements completely, Rama, Bharata's elder brother, then released the black-spotted horse, which was…” through “Then, all the great-souled Vanaras, led by Sugriva, humbly served food to the Brahmins.” through “Before a word could even emerge from the lips of the supplicants, it was already seen to be given by the monkeys and rakshasas.”

Themes: self-knowledge. The passage moves from “In that excellent sacrifice of the king, which was filled with happy and well-nourished people, there was no one who was dirty, poor, or…” through “Heaps of silver, gold, jewels, and clothes, constantly being distributed, were visible everywhere.” through “Such a sacrifice of the lion among kings, endowed with all good qualities, continued for more than a year without diminishing. इत्यार्षे…”

verses.sanskrit: तत्सर्वमखिलेनाशु प्रस्थाप्य भरताग्रजः । हयं लक्षणसम्पन्नं कृष्णसारं मुमोच ह ।। 7.92.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having quickly sent forth all those arrangements completely, Rama, Bharata's elder brother, then released the black-spotted horse, which was endowed with auspicious marks, for the sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: ऋत्विग्भिर्लक्ष्मणं सार्धमश्वतन्त्रे नियोज्य च । ततो ऽभ्यगच्छत्काकुत्स्थः सह सैन्येन नैमिशम् ।। 7.92.2 ।।
verses.translation: After appointing Lakshmana along with the priests to supervise the sacrificial horse, Rama then proceeded towards Naimisha with his army.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञवाटं महाबाहुर्दृष्ट्वा परममद्भुतम् । प्रबर्षमतुलं लेभे श्रीमानिति वचो ऽब्रवीत् ।। 7.92.3 ।।
verses.translation: Having seen the extremely wonderful sacrificial enclosure, the glorious and mighty-armed Rama felt incomparable joy and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: नैमिषे वसतस्तस्य सर्व एव नराधिपाः । आनिन्युरुपहारांश्च तान्रामः प्रत्यपूजयत् ।। 7.92.4 ।।
verses.translation: While Rama was residing in Naimisha, all the kings brought him gifts, and Rama, in turn, honored them.

verses.sanskrit: उपकार्या महार्हाश्च पार्थिवानां महात्मनाम् । सानुगानां नरश्रेष्ठो व्यादिदेश महामतिः ।। 7.92.5 ।।
verses.translation: The great-minded Rama, the best among men, arranged for valuable and suitable lodgings for the great kings and their retinues.

verses.sanskrit: अन्नपानानि वस्त्राणि सानुगानां महात्मनाम् । भरतः सन्ददावाशु शत्रुघ्नसहितस्तदा ।। 7.92.6 ।।
verses.translation: Bharata, accompanied by Shatrughna, then swiftly provided food, drinks, and clothes for the great-souled kings and their followers.

verses.sanskrit: वानराश्च महात्मानः सुग्रीवसहितास्तदा । विप्राणां प्रणताः सर्वे चक्रिरे परिवेषणम् ।। 7.92.7 ।।
verses.translation: Then, all the great-souled Vanaras, led by Sugriva, humbly served food to the Brahmins.

verses.sanskrit: बिभीषणश्च रक्षोभिर्बहुभिः स्रग्विभिर्वृतः । ऋषीणामुग्रतपसां पूजां चक्रे महात्मनाम् ।। 7.92.8 ।।
verses.translation: Vibhishana, accompanied by many garland-wearing Rakshasas, performed worship for the great-souled sages of severe penance.

verses.sanskrit: एवं सुविहितो यज्ञो हयमेधो ऽभ्यवर्तत । लक्ष्मणेनागुप्ता च हयचर्या प्रवर्तत । ईदृशं राजसिंहस्य यज्ञप्रवरमुत्तमम् ।। 7.92.9 ।।
verses.translation: Thus, the well-arranged Ashvamedha sacrifice proceeded, and the horse's journey, unguarded by Lakshmana, also took place, such was this excellent and supreme sacrifice of the lion among kings.

verses.sanskrit: नान्यः शब्दो ऽभवत्तत्र हयमेधे महात्मनः । छन्दतो देहि विस्रब्धो यावत्तुष्यन्ति याचकाः ।। 7.92.10 ।।
verses.translation: In the great-souled Rama's Ashvamedha, no other sound was heard except "Give freely and confidently until the supplicants are satisfied."

verses.sanskrit: तावत्सर्वाणि दत्तानि क्रतुमध्ये महात्मनः । विविधानि च गौडानि खाण्डवानि तथैव च ।। 7.92.11 ।।
verses.translation: In the midst of the great-souled one's sacrifice, all sorts of things were given, including various jaggery sweets and sugar sweets.
Before a word could even emerge from the lips of the supplicants, it was already seen to be given by the monkeys and rakshasas.

verses.sanskrit: न कश्चिन्मलिनस्तत्र दीनो वा ऽप्यथ कृर्शतः । तस्मिन्यज्ञवरे राज्ञो हृष्टपुष्टजनावृते ।। 7.92.13 ।।
verses.translation: In that excellent sacrifice of the king, which was filled with happy and well-nourished people, there was no one who was dirty, poor, or emaciated.

verses.sanskrit: ये च तत्र महात्मानो मुनयश्चिरजीविनः । नास्मरंस्तादृशं यज्ञं नाप्यासीत् स कदाचन ।। 7.92.14 ।।
verses.translation: Even the great-souled, long-lived sages present there had never remembered or witnessed such a sacrifice before.

verses.sanskrit: यः कृत्यवान्सुवर्णेन सुवर्णं लभते स्म सः । वित्तार्थी लभते वित्तं रत्नार्थी रत्नमेव च ।। 7.92.15 ।।
verses.translation: Whoever had a need for gold obtained gold, those desiring wealth obtained wealth, and those desiring jewels indeed obtained jewels.

verses.sanskrit: रजतानां सुवर्णानामश्मनामथ वाससाम् । अनिशं दीयमानानां राशिः समुपदृश्यते ।। 7.92.16 ।।
verses.translation: Heaps of silver, gold, jewels, and clothes, constantly being distributed, were visible everywhere.

verses.sanskrit: न शक्रस्य धनेशस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य वा । ईदृशो दृष्टपूर्वो न एवमूचुस्तपोधनाः ।। 7.92.17 ।।
verses.translation: The ascetics declared that such a spectacle had never been seen before, not even in the realms of Indra, Kubera, Yama, or Varuna.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वत्र वानरास्तस्थुः सर्वत्रैव च राक्षसाः । वासोधनान्नमर्थिभ्यः पूर्णहस्ता ददुर्भृशम् ।। 7.92.18 ।।
verses.translation: Monkeys and rakshasas were present everywhere, giving clothes, wealth, and food abundantly with full hands to all who sought them.

verses.sanskrit: ईदृशो राजसिंहस्य यज्ञः सर्वगुणान्वितः । संवत्सरमथो साग्रं वर्तते न च हीयते ।। 7.92.19 ।।
verses.translation: Such a sacrifice of the lion among kings, endowed with all good qualities, continued for more than a year without diminishing. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे द्विनवतितमः सर्गः ।। 92 ।। Thus ends the ninety-second sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
